HEARING RE: ASSIGNEE WOLVERINE ENDEAVORS VIII, LLC's Motion For Order Lifting The Stay Of Proceedings.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Wolverine Endeavors VIII, LLC's "motion for order lifting the stay of proceedings" is denied. Wolverine points to no facts, circumstances or law that have changed since the court filed the stay on June 1, 2022.

Wolverine's discovery efforts are embraced in its pending appeal. (CCP 916.) In any event, the court has broad discretion to enter stays during appeals. (Reed v. Sup. Ct. (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 448, 455.) Pursuant to CCP section 703.580(b), Defendants have the burden of proof. "The claim of exemption is deemed controverted by the notice of opposition to the claim of exemption and both shall be received in evidence. If no other evidence is offered, the court, if satisfied that sufficient facts are shown by the claim of exemption (including the financial statement if one is required) and the notice of opposition, may make its determination thereon. If not satisfied, the court shall order the hearing continued for the production of other evidence, oral or documentary." (Code of Civil Procedure, section 703.580(c).) On the claim form, Defendants indicate that the account consists of private social security benefits, citing CCP section 704.080(c) and CCP section 704.220(b). However, this Court has no information to determine if these exceptions apply because the Defendants failed to provide any evidence or facts to support their claim.

Off Calendar Apr-04-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. NICK I. IEZZA, Esq. and RACHEL PARENT, Esq. of Spiwak & Iezza, LLP (Tel: 805-777-1175) for Assignee of Record Wolverine Endeavors VIII.
Atty. ITAK K. MORADI, Esq. of Knox Ricksen LLP (Tel: 925-433-2500) for proposed interventor AKRO Real Estate Partners, LLC;
Atty. MICHAEL D. HAUPT, Esq. of Glick Haupt Marino LLP (Tel: 805-544-2450) for John King and Carol King.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR AKRO REAL ESTATE PARTNERS LLC's AKRO Real Estate Partners LLC's Motion For Leave To Intervene.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

AKRO Real Estate Partners LLC's ("AKRO") motion for leave to intervene is granted.

"The purpose of intervention is to promote fairness by involving all parties potentially affected by a judgment in the litigation." (Simpson Redwood Co. v. State of California (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 1192, 1199.) Moreover, the Court liberally construes CCP sec. 387 in favor of intervention. (Id. at 1200 ["And section 387 should be liberally construed in favor of intervention."] Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387(b), "if the person seeking intervention claims an interest relating to the property or transaction which is the subject of the action and that person is so situated that the disposition of the action may as a practical matter impair or impede that person's ability to protect that interest, unless that person's interest is adequately represented by existing parties, the court shall, upon timely application, permit that person to intervene." As the assignee of a financial judgment obtained in 2010 by Textron Financial Corporation against Defendants John E. King and Carole King (collectively "Defendants"), AKRO has a direct interest in this litigation and protecting its right to recover from Defendants. Therefore, the Court finds a sound basis for mandatory intervention under Code of Civil Procedure section 387(b).

The Court rejects assignee Wolverine Endeavors VIII, a California limited liability company's ("Wolverine") argument that AKRO's lien is invalid. According to Cal. Code Civ. Proc. section 708.110, the "judgment creditor shall personally serve a copy of the order on the judgment debtor not less than 10 days before the date set for the examination. Service shall be made in the manner specified in Section 415.10. Service of the order creates a lien on the personal property of the judgment debtor for a period of one year from the date of the order unless extended or sooner terminated by the court." (Cal. Code Civ. Proc., sec. 708.110(d).) Moreover, the proper court for such an examination is the court in which the judgment was entered, unless the person sought to be examined does not reside in the county where the judgment was entered, in which case the superior court in the county where the person resides is a proper court of examination of the debtor. (Cal. Code Civ. Proc., Sction Code 708.160(c).) In this case, AKRO created a valid lien on Defendant's property by filing an order of examination in the superior court of the county of the debtor's residence, San Luis Obispo County, and serving it on Defendant John E. King. AKRO then filed for its order and the order was issued on November 3, 2021. AKRO then served John E. King with the order on November 16, 2021.

Finally, the Court rejects Wolverine's argument that the motion should be denied because it was filed in an untimely manner. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697 ["It is well settled that the appearance of a party at the hearing of a motion and his or her opposition to the motion on its merits a waiver of any defects or irregularities in the notice of motion. This rule applies even when no notice was given at all."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
